mission.title: kyverno: [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing Namespaced Policy
mission.description: [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing Namespaced Policy. This issue affects 11+ users.

steps.title: Identify kyverno troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kyverno deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
Kyverno appears to perform some level of processing of all Pods across the cluster when matching against a Namespaced Policy where the `match` block does not specify a Namespace and the rule is a mutate existing rule.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate existing…
steps.description:

### Proof Manifests
```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
  name: kyverno:update-pods
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - ""
  resources:
  - pods
  verbs:
  - update
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug] Kyverno processes all Pods in a mutate… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno -n kyverno --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
*: If this PR results in new or altered behavior which is user facing, you

read and follow the steps outlined in the [PR documentation guide](pr_documentation.md) and add Proof Manifests as defined below.
-->

### Proof Manifests
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
  name: kyverno:update-pods
rules:
- apiGroups:
  - ""
  resources:
  - pods
  verbs:
  - update

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: Policy
metadata:
  name: label-pod-test-1
  namespace: default
spec:
  mutateExistingOnPolicyUpdate: true
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: add-label
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
          names:
          - "test-1-*"
    mutate:
      targets:
      - apiVersion: v1
        kind: Pod
        name: "{{ request.object.metadata.name }}"
        namespace: default
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          labels:
            my-label: "true"
# Kyverno CLI test manifest (please see docs for latest manifest format at https://kyverno.io/docs/kyverno-cli/). See kyverno/policies for complete examples of all related test files.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
